description: Twilio WhatsApp Content template metadata v2 for the Patient Retention Engine. Runtime variables map into Twilio ContentVariables as {{1}}, {{2}}, etc.

templates.trigger: Patient completes QR intake and joins the doctor queue
templates.message:
Hi {{patient_name}},

You're now in {{doctor_name}}'s queue at {{clinic_name}}.

We'll send your prescription and any care updates to this number. See you inside.
templates.notes: v2 welcome after QR intake (WF7). {{1}} patient, {{2}} doctor, {{3}} clinic.

templates.message: Hi {{hospital_name}}, your onboarding for {{facility_type}} is complete. We have registered {{doctor_name}} as the primary contact and {{city}} as the facility location. Reply CONFIRM if this is correct or EDIT if you need changes.

templates.trigger: Doctor issues prescription without follow-up (short course)
templates.message:
Hi {{patient_name}}, your prescription from {{doctor_name}} is attached.

Please take your medicines as prescribed — the timings are listed inside.

If you have any questions, call us at the clinic.

Take care.
templates.notes: PDF sent via MediaUrl after template. {{1}} patient, {{2}} doctor.

templates.trigger: Doctor issues prescription with follow-up scheduled
templates.message:
Hi {{patient_name}}, your prescription from {{doctor_name}} is attached.

{{doctor_name}} would like to see you again on {{follow_up_date}}. We'll remind you before then.

Follow your medicines in the meantime. It makes a real difference.

Hi {{patient_name}}, your prescription from {{doctor_name}} is attached.

Your health journey starts today.

{{doctor_name}} has prescribed a {{course_days}}-day course. Each day you take your medicine, you're one step closer to feeling like yourself again.

Call us at the clinic if you have any questions.

Good morning {{patient_name}}.

Day 1 of your health journey — time for your {{medicine_name}} before breakfast.

The first step is always the most important. You've got this.

Good evening {{patient_name}}.

{{medicine_name}} after dinner tonight — and that's Day 1 done.

How are you feeling so far? Keep going — {{doctor_name}} is rooting for you.

Hi {{patient_name}}, {{clinic_name}} checking in.

You're halfway through your {{course_days}}-day course — great going.

This is exactly where most people start feeling the difference. Keep your medicines consistent and your health takes care of itself.

Good morning {{patient_name}}.

Time for your {{medicine_name}} — another day, another step forward.

How do you think the journey is going? {{doctor_name}}'s clinic is with you all the way.

Good morning {{patient_name}}.

Last day of your {{course_days}}-day course — just this dose and you've done it.

{{doctor_name}} will be pleased to hear you stayed consistent. Take your {{medicine_name}} as prescribed and rest well today.

templates.trigger: Course complete evening (20:00 on last day)
templates.message:
Hi {{patient_name}}, you've completed your full {{course_days}}-day course prescribed by {{doctor_name}}.

That takes real commitment — well done.

If you're feeling much better, that's great. If anything still feels off, call the clinic and {{doctor_name}} will take another look.

Good morning {{patient_name}}.

Time for your {{medicine_name}} — please take it before breakfast, as {{doctor_name}} prescribed.

Small habits. Steady health.

Hi {{patient_name}}, your {{medicine_name}} is due after lunch today.

{{clinic_name}} checking in. Keep at it.

Good evening {{patient_name}}.

Don't forget your {{medicine_name}} after dinner tonight.

Rest well — you're doing the right things.

templates.trigger: Day(s) before follow-up (WF1 daily 09:00, follow_up_date = tomorrow)
templates.message:
Hi {{patient_name}},

Your follow-up with {{doctor_name}} is coming up on {{follow_up_date}}.

This visit matters for your health. We'll remind you again the morning before.

Please confirm using the button below.
templates.notes: WF1 primary template with Confirm/Reschedule buttons. WF6 handles taps and queue creation.

templates.message:
Good morning {{patient_name}}.

Your follow-up with {{doctor_name}} is today.

{{clinic_name}} is open. {{doctor_name}} is expecting you. See you soon.

Hi {{patient_name}}, this is {{clinic_name}} — just checking in.

It's been a few days since your visit. We hope you're feeling better.

If you need anything, call us at the clinic anytime.

templates.trigger: Same day after missed follow-up (+1 day overdue)
templates.message:
Hi {{patient_name}}, we noticed you couldn't make it today. We hope everything is okay.

Whenever you're ready, call us at the clinic and we'll find a time that works for you.

Hi {{patient_name}}, {{clinic_name}} checking in again.

{{doctor_name}} still has your visit in mind. It's worth coming in before too long.

Call us at the clinic whenever you're ready — we'll sort a time for you.

Hi {{patient_name}}, your follow-up with {{doctor_name}} is confirmed for {{follow_up_date}}.

{{doctor_name}} will be ready for you. We'll send a reminder the evening before.

See you then.

Hi {{patient_name}}, no problem at all — your visit with {{doctor_name}} has been moved to {{follow_up_date}}.

We'll remind you the morning before.

See you soon.
templates.notes: Sent when patient taps Reschedule; staff follows up to set new date. follow_up_date may be TBD placeholder until rescheduled.

templates.trigger: Session fallback when booking-confirmed template unavailable
templates.message: Thank you, {{name}}! You're confirmed for {{follow_up_date}}. We've added you to the queue. See you then!

templates.trigger: Session fallback when reschedule template unavailable
templates.message: Hi {{name}}, no problem at all! We've noted that you'd like to reschedule. A member of our team will reach out shortly to arrange a new time.

twilio_template_categories.UTILITY: Transactional care messages tied to intake, prescription, reminders, or care plan.
twilio_template_categories.MARKETING: Re-engagement or promotional messages. Confirm opt-in before production.
